title: HOW MANY DIGITS DO I HAVE

content.problemStatement:
Write a program to obtain a number (N)(N)(N) from the user and display whether the number is a one digit number, 2 digit number, 3 digit number or more than 3 digit number


Input Format
First line will contain the number NNN,



Output Format
Print "1" if N is a 1 digit number.


Print "2" if N is a 2 digit number.


Print "3" if N is a 3 digit number.


Print "More than 3 digits" if N has more than 3 digits.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: Print the number of digits in N. If N has 1 digit, print '1'. If N has 2 digits, print '2'. If N has 3 digits, print '3'. If N has more than 3 digits, print 'More than 3 digits'.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The number 1000 has more than 3 digits.

solution.approach: To find the number of digits in N, we can convert N to a string and then find the length of the string. Alternatively, we can use a mathematical approach by repeatedly dividing N by 10 until it becomes 0 and counting the number of divisions.
solution.timeComplexity: O(log N) — because we are repeatedly dividing N by 10
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — because we are using a constant amount of space
